title: Liverpool rebuff Real's Alexander-Arnold approach - Wednesday's gossip
content:
Daily Mail back page

The Time back page

The Guardian back page

The Sun back page

Daily Express back page

Daily Mirror back page

1 of 6

Liverpool rebuff Real Madrid's approach for Trent Alexander-Arnold, Juventus and Napoli want to sign Joshua Zirkzee, while Mohamed Salah and Virgil van Dijk are set to stay at Liverpool.

Liverpoolhave turned down an approach fromReal Madridto sign England right-back Trent Alexander-Arnold, 26, in January.(Athletic - subscription required),external

JuventusandNapoliare interested in signingManchester United's23-year-old Netherlands striker, Joshua Zirkzee.(Mail),external

Manchester Unitedhave no budget to reinforce their squad in January and would have to sell first before buying to comply with profitability and sustainability and Financial Fair Play rules.(Manchester Evening News),external

Egypt winger Mohamed Salah, 32, and Netherlands defender Virgil van Dijk, 33, are both expected to extend theirLiverpoolstays by two years.(David Ornstein, via Mirror),external

Liverpoolare open to selling Uruguay striker Darwin Nunez, 25, and have identifiedInter Milanand France forward Marcus Thuram, 27, along withNewcastle'sSweden striker Alexander Isak, 25, as potential replacements.(Teamtalk),external

Liverpoolcould rivalManchester Unitedfor the signing of 21-year-old Hungary left-back Milos Kerkez from Bournemouth.(Caught Offside),external

Get the latest from your club

Listen to the latest Football Daily podcast

Wolvesare weighing up a bid forMillwall's25-year-old  Englishdefender Japhet Tanganga in January.(Express),external

Nottingham Forestare confident of agreeing a new contract with 28-year-old Nigeria full-back Ola Aina, who is a target forManchester City.(Football Insider),external

Real Madridare interested inBournemouth'sSpain under-21 defender Dean Huijsen, 19.(Fichajes - in Spanish),external

Arsenal,Liverpool,ChelseaandTottenhamhave joinedManchester UnitedandManchester Cityin showing interest inBarcelona's26-year-old Spain forward Dani Olmo.(Mundo Deportivo - in Spanish),external

Barcelonacould make a loan move forChelsea's27-year-old France forward Christopher Nkunku if they fail to register Olmo for the second half of the season.(Caught Offside),external

Olmo, however, wants to stay atBarcelona, despite uncertainty over his registration.(Fabrizio Romano, via GiveMeSport),external

Bayern Munichhave shortlisted a number of goalkeepers who they could sign this summer, includingBrighton's22-year-old Netherlands international Bart Verbruggen.(Bild - in German, subscription required),external

Chelseaare willing to sell 21-year-old Italian midfielder Cesare Casadei, who has attracted interest from Italian sideTorino.(Talksport),external
